text:
Once upon a time in a forest deep and dense, there lived an animal unlike any other. This animal had eyes the color of twilight clouds and wings that shimmered like amber. They called this creature Sparklewing.

Every morning, Sparklewing would soar through the dappled sunlight, leaving glitter trails behind. The animals of the forest adored this magical bird. They would gather around its perch to watch the shimmering patterns and listen to its gentle songs.

But Sparklewing had a unique curse. Every year, on the eve before Valentine's Day, their wings turned a dull gray. They knew that meant farewell to shimmering flights for a whole year. They would hide away in secret places, avoiding eyes.

This year, Sparklewing had retreated to the deepest part of a crystal-clear lake. The animals were crestfallen, but as dawn approached, they saw a glimmer of hope. A small boy noticed Sparklewing perched on a lily-pad and,
